footnotes.text: From this it seems that the Christians were accustomed to receive Baptism externally as substitutes for the catechumens who had received it only in desire. It did not have sacramental effect, but was tolerated as being the performance of an act the catechumens desired but could not themselves receive. It showed a belief in the Resurrection.
Quotation taken from "Thais," a play by Menander.
The meaning would be that while those who are living at the last day will not die, they must undergo the change spoken of in the previous verses, from the natural body to the spiritual body.
